description: Redesign unconfigured plugin cards so they are forced open (expanded, non-collapsible) with the Configure button always visible. Remove the subtle 'Needs Setup' badge and gear icon from the header — the expanded state itself is the indicator.

userStories.title: Remove Needs Setup badge and make unconfigured cards non-collapsible in PluginCard
userStories.description: Remove the 'Needs Setup' badge + Settings icon from the PluginCard header. Disable the accordion trigger and hide the chevron when the plugin is unconfigured. The Configure button in the accordion content (already exists) becomes the primary CTA, always visible because the card is forced open.
acceptanceCriteria: The 13-line block rendering the Needs Setup badge + Settings icon + Tooltip (lines 259-271) is removed from PluginCard.tsx
AccordionPrimitive.Trigger has disabled={isUnconfigured} and cursor-default class when unconfigured
ChevronDown is conditionally rendered: hidden when isUnconfigured is true
The Configure button block (lines 310-317) is unchanged — still renders when isUnconfigured
The Settings import from lucide-react remains (used by the Configure button at line 314)
Badge import remains (used by the DEV badge at line 247)
userStories.notes: Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx Context: The current PluginCard header renders three optional indicators after the plugin name: DEV badge (line 246-250), unreviewed shield icon (line 251-258), and the Needs Setup badge (line 259-271). We are removing only the Needs Setup block. Changes: 1. **Remove the Needs Setup block** (lines 259-271). Delete the entire `{isUnconfigured && (` block through its closing `)}`. The code to remove is: ```tsx {isUnconfigured && ( Needs Setup This plugin has required settings that are not configured )} ``` 2. **Disable the accordion trigger** when unconfigured. Change line 242 from: ```tsx ``` to: ```tsx svg]:rotate-180 ${isUnconfigured ? 'cursor-default' : 'cursor-pointer'}`}> ``` 3. **Hide the chevron** when unconfigured. Change lines 273-275 from: ```tsx ``` to: ```tsx {!isUnconfigured && ( )} ``` Constraints: - Do NOT remove the `isUnconfigured` variable (line 154) — it's still used by the accordion content (line 310) and the trigger disabled prop - Do NOT remove the `needsSetup` import from ConfigDialog — still used at line 154 - Do NOT remove `Settings` from the lucide-react import — still used by the Configure button at line 314 - Do NOT remove `Badge` import — still used by the DEV badge at line 247 - Do NOT change the accordion content area (lines 309-339) — the Configure button stays as-is - Do NOT change the PluginMenu Settings item — it's the fallback for post-configuration edits

userStories.title: Force unconfigured plugins open in PluginList accordion state
userStories.description: In PluginList, compute which plugins need setup per group and force them into the controlled accordion value arrays. Wrap onValueChange to prevent users from collapsing unconfigured cards.
acceptanceCriteria: needsSetup is imported from ConfigDialog in PluginList.tsx
Unconfigured plugin names are computed per group (unconfiguredReady, unconfiguredNotReady)
effectiveOpenReady and effectiveOpenNotReady merge forced-open names into the user-controlled arrays
Accordion value props use the effective arrays instead of raw openReady/openNotReady
onValueChange handlers re-add forced-open names so unconfigured plugins cannot be collapsed
Configured plugins can still be expanded and collapsed normally

userStories.title: Update E2E test assertion and CLAUDE.md documentation
userStories.description: Update the plugin-settings E2E test that asserts 'Needs Setup' text visibility, and update the browser-extension CLAUDE.md to document the new forced-open behavior instead of the removed badge.
acceptanceCriteria: The E2E assertion at plugin-settings.e2e.ts:247 no longer checks for 'Needs Setup' text (badge was removed)
The assertion is replaced with a check that the Configure button is visible (since unconfigured cards are forced open)
The 'NeedsSetup badge' paragraph in platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md is replaced with documentation of the forced-open behavior
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/plugin-settings.e2e.ts (line 247)
- platform/browser-extension/CLAUDE.md (line 168)

Context:

**E2E test change:**
The test at line 247 currently asserts:
```ts
// Verify NeedsSetup badge is NOT shown (all configSchema fields are optional)
await expect(sidePanelPage.getByText('Needs Setup')).not.toBeVisible();
```

Since the 'Needs Setup' text no longer exists anywhere in the UI, this assertion will trivially pass (element not found = not visible). But it's now testing a removed feature. Replace with a meaningful assertion. Since the e2e-test plugin has only optional configSchema fields, it should NOT be forced open — so verify the Configure button is NOT visible (the card should be collapsed with its normal tool list):
```ts
// Verify unconfigured state is NOT shown (all configSchema fields are optional)
await expect(sidePanelPage.getByRole('button', { name: 'Configure' })).not.toBeVisible();
```

Note: Read the surrounding test context (lines 210-267) to understand the full test — it opens the side panel, waits for the e2e-test plugin card to appear, and checks various settings behaviors. The assertion at line 247 is in the 'side panel shows Settings menu item' test.

**CLAUDE.md change:**
Replace the paragraph at line 168:
```
**NeedsSetup badge**: `PluginCard` shows a Settings icon + "Needs Setup" badge in the plugin card header when `needsSetup(plugin)` is true. When the badge is present, the card content area shows a "Configure" button instead of the tool list, prompting the user to open `ConfigDialog`.
```

With:
```
**Unconfigured state**: When `needsSetup(plugin)` is true, the plugin card is forced open (expanded by default, cannot be collapsed). The accordion trigger and chevron are disabled, and the content area shows a "Configure" button instead of the tool list. This makes the setup CTA immediately visible without requiring user interaction.
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other assertions in plugin-settings.e2e.ts
- Do NOT change any other paragraphs in CLAUDE.md
- The e2eCheckpoint is true on this story because it's the final story and touches browser-observable behavior
